review_text:
9/10 times your order would be successfully delivered. Hopefully it won't go missing and hopefully the fit would be perfect! 
Now comes the issue when your order doesn't get to you because it's lost in transit, or your order doesn't fit right or doesn't look right etc. this is when you'll be dealing with their awful customer service and you'll get one heck of a bad experience. You'd be wishing you hadn't ordered with them in the first place.
And you won't get any gestures of goodwill from them as they don't care. This is the company that doesn't like free returns either annd monitors how many returns you made before they charge you for returning ann item. They want you to think twice and carefully  before ordering from them and that's what I'd be doing. Thinking about where else I  can buy a certain product from. 
 In a nutshell that's the company asos is.

Order number 1036371837. I received a notification that my package had been delivered by in post to the local collection point. I went to collect it today and was told that the courier had taken it back even though I have until tomorrow to collect it! Hope been trying to use a chat but to resolve this but I just keep going round in a circle

I’m extremely disappointed with the service I’ve received. My parcel was not delivered to me personally, but instead left in a communal post room. This area is not secure, and as a result, I never received my item.

Despite this, I’ve been refused a refund. I paid using Clearpay, so I don’t have Section 75 protection, which makes the situation even more frustrating. Regardless of the payment method, the responsibility to ensure safe and correct delivery still lies with the retailer.

From my perspective, you have failed to uphold your delivery contract. I paid for goods that I did not receive, yet I am now left out of pocket with no resolution.

This feels completely unfair. I would strongly urge you to review this case properly and provide the refund that I am entitled to.

I will be using the small claims court if I do not hear back

Would give zero stars if I could. Package was delivered to a random bush on another street and even then I was only able to gain a photo of where it was actually delivered by raising the issue with DAI since they did not provide it upon notification of "delivery". ASOS also used Evri on the UK side and their customer service was useless (AI slop). ASOS customer service was AI slop as well. Extraordinarily poor service.

Have had a few products from asos now, always arrived in good time, at a good price. 
Just ordered a new jacket from them.

I returned an item and did not receive a refund. I spent ages in their live chat trying to speak to a human and then was connected with an agent to whom I provided all the information I could, including proof of shipping, and she closed down the chat without helping me. There is no other way to contact them. They will steal your money and refuse to help you.
Zero customer service. Send incorrect items and expect you to pay to return them.

I had a really disappointing experience with this company. I ordered a dress (order #1033449625), but was away on a work trip when it arrived. As soon as I got back, I tried it on and found it didn’t fit.

Although I was still within the 28-day return period, I discovered I couldn’t generate a return label anymore because their system had already closed that option. I reached out to customer service, explained the situation, and asked for help—but they refused to make any exception.

It’s frustrating that a company would hide behind a technicality instead of showing any flexibility or understanding, especially when the request is reasonable and within the overall return window. This kind of rigid and unhelpful customer service really damages trust.

I won’t be ordering from them again.
Terrible company,The company scammed me when they said they will process the refund, and the live agents over 4 days 3 times when they assured me they will process it

I’ve always had a great experience with ASOS but recently I had to return two items and did not get my refund. I contacted their online chat three days in a row and was finally told my refund was processed but i had to wait another ten days (even though I’ve been waiting for eleven days) to get my money. I have subscription to free delivery until July but after that I’m not renewing and I’ll bin them .. sorry that is not good customer service in my opinion. I’m sad as I thought they were pretty good until now.

Hey this review goes for  Asos.com  clothing company based in UK. I ordered  my first and second  items from this company and everything was fine and good.  Got my items by paying for my Duties and Taxes through my country's postal service,  That's  Canada Post. This year l decided to order  some items from this same company.  My first time  was around  January and February,  Every item was checked out and paid and received  with a tracking number  Though this scam shipping company known as Asendia. This shipping company then started sending me messages that I Should pay Duties and Taxes on my parcel but they Don't tell me where and how to go to pay for the duty and tax.  No invoice or takets  was sent to me to go and pay for Duties and Taxes.  No phone calls for me to pay for the duty and tax on the phone or online. All l read was my orders are been sent back to the retailer and this issue has happened twice within February and March  this year.  I have been trying to get ASOS.COM TO TALK TO SOMEONE TO EXPLAIN WHAT IS GOING ON WITH THE SHIPPING COMPANY THEY ARE USING TO SHIP  THEIR  PRODUCTS TO CANADA THAT WE ARE NOT GETTING OUR PARCELS AND ORDERS SO ASOS SHOULD REFUND MY MONEY BACK TO ME WITH BOTH OF THE ORDERS. ASOS HAS PROVEN TO BE ANOTHER USELESS COMPANY WHEN IT COMES TO COMMUNICATION. BOTH COMPANIES SUCKS WITH COMMUNICATION AND CUSTOMER SERVICE  THEY SHOULD RECONSTRUCT THEIR COMMUNICATIONS SYSTEMS SO CUSTOMERS CAN TRUST THEM MORE BECAUSE WE WANT TO SHOP AND DON'T LOSE OUR MONEY.  SO FAR I HAVE LOST A LOT OF MONEY WITH ASOS AND I DON'T THINK THEY ARE EVEN AWARE OF WHAT IS GOING ON  TO EVEN  REFUND MY MONEY BACK TO ME  BOTH ITEMS  PURCHASED FROM ASOS.COM WERE $160 AND $362 CANADIAN  DOLLARS.  SO JUST IMAGINE HOW MANY PEOPLE HAD GONE THROUGH THIS WITH THIS COMPANY. ASOS YOU SHOULD CHANGE YOUR SHIPPING COMPANY BECAUSE YOUR CUSTOMERS IN CANADA ARE NOT HAPPY. WE ARE NOT GETTING OUR PARCELS AND ORDERS AND YOU ARE NOT REFUNDING US BACK OUT MONEY PLUS YOUR CUSTOMER SERVICE IS THE WORST.  CUSTOMERS PLEASE BE CAREFUL WITH THIS TO USELESS COMPANYS.

If I could give 0 stars I would , ordered on 17th or march some stuff for my holidays paid next day delivery as on 19th I had a flight arranged to leave someone home to collect the delivery then I get an email from Royal Mail say”attempted to deliver your parcel but no answer” when they did not even try as someone was home . Contacted their customer service which is an AI and is a joke , got to an agent finally explained everything as obviously I did not need them anymore first because nobody would be home and second they were for holidays initially he said they can see is still out for delivery when I did send a screen from the Royal Mail email I had received he said that confusing however wait until end of the day and we will issue a refund if you contact us you did not get it I am still contacting them and nothing yet . Avoid!!!!!

I placed an order over a week ago, 10 days ago, was advised delivery 3-5 working days, the new delivery provider “Relay” have lost my parcel and now unable to provide a date. I have tried to liaise with Customer Sevice at ASOS, to be put on hold and unable to raise a complaint or receive I refund. This is very poor for ASOS.
ASOS you ain’t sh**. You sent my shoes in a smashed up box. I mean it literally looks like someone grabbed the shoe box w their hands and smashed it. like a hulk squeeze.

ordered yesterday with next day delivery, hasn’t even been dispatched. been told to wait until 10pm tonight to see if it gets delivered before they will be willing to help.. if it hasn’t been dispatched then it’s clearly not getting delivered today!
Awful customer service, if you ever manage to actually get a response.

Ordered adidas spezials for daughter bday. 
Left insole not stuck in properly,  right insole only partially stuck in , for £90 I expect it to be perfect

Gone downhill, especially the customer service. The chat takes ages - it's like they're trying to wait you out until you give up on them responding. They don't even send emails any more. 

Oh and both my next-day delivery orders came late: one came one day late and the other was two days late.

Be careful of a site called asos uk flash sales its a scam in which unfortunately I've learnt, but bank will reimburse me. So before buying anything if you dont know the company is to check out their address. I checked it to late the company comes up as boohoo on this site address in London. Boohoo is  actually in Manchester. The company vat number on this scam site does not exist to the company and neither does the email address. So be careful while shopping online. I normally am but on this 1 occasion I wasn't.

I can only echo everyone else here I paid for premier delivery service ordered an item for a wedding and it never turned up. The tracking has not even updated and i contacted them to check and they kept telling me check tomorrow. I asked for refund on the premier as the delivery has not turned up and they have fobbed me off 'you will be contacted about it' when i asked when that would be they said there isnt a time frame! the agents just fob you off and literally tell lies and I feel they have stolen my money!

I'm truly disappointed.
I have ordered on Monday a large package ,I paid 400 euros,plus 25 euros to have next day delivery. I was supposed to get my parcel today ,but I received an email saying my parcel is delayed. I paid next day delivery for nothing...

I have been a loyal shopper with ASOS for over 15 20 years I believe I am absolutely disgusted that this is the fourth week and the ninth chat that I have had to go to with your customer care team to try and get my money back money that for some reason you will not give me back even though you have got your products back you are refusing my money on two orders £152 is what you owe me I have been promised over and over and over that this will be sorted and I am ignored over and over and over I have had your customer care team closed chats on me because they are sick of me going on which is disgusting I have sent bank statements in to show that this money has not gone back into my account yet I am still here trying to get my money how dare you treat customers like this how day your customer care team be so disgusting and ignorant when you're trying to get help when you're frustrated and angry because you've come back again and again and again to try and chase promises that have been broken to try and get people to listen to you because you don't have the money that you've been told that you've been sent I am disgusted and appalled and I am going to take this further I am going to find a way to contact head office I have got all my chat logs I have got all the numbers I've got all the names of people that I've spoken to over the four weeks that I've been trying to chase my refunds and I am going to take this further I am disgusted with such a big company and how appallingly I have been treated

I have entered two chat logs the other people wouldn't give me chat logs that again is an appalling thing to do someone called Timone actually close the chat on me because I was trying to explain how many times I've been back and they literally ended the chat because well I don't even know why you would have to ask him to say I am absolutely utterly disgraced at how you have treated me is an understatement

I can now understand why ASOS has such a low rating on Trustpilot – my recent experience reflects exactly why.

I was charged £3.95 for returns after being told my return rate is 74%, but this completely ignores the context. I had to place multiple orders for Christmas jumpers for my father because the sizing was inconsistent and incorrect. Like most customers, I cannot know how something will fit until it is tried on, especially with clothing and shoes.

What’s most frustrating is how this has been handled. I attempted to raise a complaint and found it very difficult to get through on live chat. I was given a reference number (1206893), but when I tried to follow up via email, the message bounced back saying their mailbox was full and unable to accept messages.

After finally receiving a response from the escalations team, my situation was not properly considered. Instead, I was given a generic explanation of size guides and then told that any further emails on the matter would be closed without response.

This is not acceptable complaint handling. Customers should be able to raise concerns properly and have them fairly reviewed, especially when charges are being applied in situations outside of their control.

Due to this experience, I will not be shopping with ASOS again and will be raising this issue further with Citizens Advice.


UPDATE: It has now been several days and I have received no response from ASOS, either here or via email. I have already raised a complaint (ref 1206893) and made multiple attempts to resolve this, but have been unable to get a proper response. This only adds to my concerns about how customer complaints are handled.

They have good items, modern, but pricey. The issue, however, is their customer service and there processes. They apparently have a business model that does not care about you once you have made payment.

My order was split into two parcels and one of the parcels has never arrived. I have been in contact with customer care multiple times over the past three weeks and have repeatedly been told that an investigation has been raised. However, during subsequent chats I have been told that no investigation exists and the process has been restarted.The value of the missing item is significant and it is unacceptable that after three weeks there is still no resolution.

Under consumer law, as the retailer you remain responsible for the delivery of the goods until they reach the customer. As the item has not been delivered, I am requesting a full refund immediately.

ASOS is a fraud and their customer service is non-existent. I made a few returns and have not received my funds back. I basically lost money and when contacting their customer service all I speak to is AI as no real human being seems to be there anymore. No resolution provided and no money back for the returned items. It's a fraud website, beware from buying clothes there. If you return you might not get money. No email address provided where I can send my complaint and AI on their customer service chat is useless. So disappointed and feel helpless. I'm looking for legal steps I can take to get help.

Wish I could rate lower than one star!
Received a faulty item, the online chatbot is useless, they then offer to put you through to a person - well what a joke that is!
No issue with sending my item back, it’s the sheer lack of customer service and offer of discount codes that don’t work on anything! 
Truly, shop elsewhere as this company is a joke!

ASOS customer service refuses to provide anything close to customer service. 

Not only do they refuse to offer any phone support (I’m dyslexic and struggle with communicating through  also due to a neurological disorder) for a reasonable adjustment meaning they’re in violation of the 2010 Equalities Act. They don’t care about disabled people. 

Trying to talk to them I was on their awful chat portal for well over an hour, explaining or trying to explain over and over, when I finally got through to a supervisor (I had an issue with items for a funeral that were supposed to be delivered for a week, got confirmed lost the day before I leave), I waited a while - they copy pasted the same message to me three times over and then the next thing I know they said a specialist team would email me. 

Super unhelpful.
Pathetic customer service and the chat box ai thing is a waste of a consumers time and the agents don’t reply on the chat..ignore u! Will never use asos again!!! Pathetic!!!

I’m honestly so upset and disappointed with this company. I returned a pair of shorts that were not worn, yet they’ve falsely claimed they were “heavily worn” and refused to refund me. Even after escalating this to management ( which took two weeks just to respond) they still dismissed my case.

The shorts sent back to me are clearly in perfect condition, aside from a stitching fault which is obviously a manufacturing issue. The photo they provided allegedly showing a mark is completely misleading - when I received the item back, there was no mark at all. This raises serious concerns about their returns checks.

I am a single parent already struggling financially. I used Klarna to responsibly manage payments for an occasion, only keeping what I needed and returning the rest. Because of their refusal to refund, I’ve now been left unable to cover essential bills.

This has caused me so much stress and has genuinely affected my mental health due to not being able to pay bills.

I would strongly advise others to think twice before ordering from this company. Based on my experience, their returns process is unreliable and their customer service is extremely disappointing.

I honestly will never order from ASOS again and my family and friends have said the same, and I will be advising everyone I know to do the same.

My parcel arrived damaged and was missing 2 items. When I requested a refund for the missing items, they closed my account for "suspicious activity". I contacted customer service and they were of no help. 

Before that, I ordered a bag and a t-shirt and they sent me two of the same t-shirts and no bag. People wouldn't have to make refund claims if they packed the orders correctly. The lack of accountability from them is outstanding.

I bought an expensive pair of shoes and a few hours after they were 20% off they refused to give me a price adjustment or cancel my order to led me to rebuy them and return the full price ones once I receive them and would refund me 20 days later!! I feel like they track what people put in their carts this has halftime so many people I know!
Scammers. Closing my account for no reason, after i reached out regarding a missing item in my package.

I rarely leave reviews, but this experience genuinely forced me to.

I ordered a pair of Fred Perry shoes which arrived in perfect condition. The issue is that they did not fit, and due to circumstances I was only able to properly try them after the standard 14-day return period.

The shoes are completely new – only tried on indoors, no wear, no damage, fully resellable. Despite this, ASOS refuses to provide any solution and continues to respond with what appear to be automated or scripted messages.

What is most frustrating is not even the policy itself, but the complete lack of flexibility or human consideration in a situation where a reasonable exception would cost them nothing, yet makes a huge difference for the customer.

I have contacted support multiple times and have not received any real assistance – only repeated generic responses. This gives the impression that customer satisfaction is not actually a priority once the purchase has been completed.

It is disappointing to see a company of this size handle such a straightforward situation in this way. I hope someone from ASOS reviews this properly and resolves it, because right now this reflects very poorly on their customer service.

Have ring doorbell footage of the parcel not being left, they issued a replacement without my consent, I needed the items next day, hence why I also paid for the premier delivery. The refund email telling me it’s processed is nowhere to be seen. I used to shop here relentlessly, it was my go to shop online. Stay well clear, returns are monitored, and they keep your money and the customer service is terrible.

Painful to deal with if you need to return a faulty item. Still waiting for a refund since the beginning of January. No one to talk to and the Ai chat is shockingly bad. No idea how to even escalate this as I’m just waisting my own time.

ASOS confirmed they received my returned items, yet I’m now being chased by debt collectors for them.

This has been ongoing since June of last year, despite multiple attempts to resolve it.

I’ve raised complaints and even sent a formal legal letter with no response or resolution from ASOS.

This is now putting my credit file at risk, despite ASOS acknowledging they have the goods.

A really disappointing experience. I’d strongly recommend being cautious when ordering multiple items or using Klarna with ASOS.

ASOS - please resolve this urgently.

Unless you are actually in the UK, don't buy from here, the hidden fees for import duties are ridiculous, you'll basically be paying double what they claim in shipping, even when they claim that it's free shipping (it's really not).

I’m extremely disappointed with the way ASOS has handled a quality issue with one of their own products.

I purchased a pair of boots from the ASOS Design range at the beginning of December. Within less than two months of normal wear, both soles had completely split, which is clearly a quality issue.

I contacted customer service via chat and was asked to send photos, which I did. After reviewing them, I was instructed to send the boots back to ASOS for inspection and was provided with a shipping label. This was not a return request from me – I was simply following their instructions so they could assess the fault.

About a week after sending them back, I received an automated email saying my “return” had been rejected because it was outside the return period. This made no sense given the boots were sent back specifically for inspection after a complaint about their quality.

I contacted customer service again to explain the situation. Initially I received generic responses repeating the automated message. On my third attempt, I was told the issue had been escalated and that someone would contact me within 24–48 hours.

No one ever contacted me.

Instead, about a week later the broken boots simply turned up back in the post with no explanation, no resolution, and no communication at all.

I have since tried to contact ASOS again, but each time there appears to be no record of my previous conversations, meaning I have had to explain the situation over and over again. After the fifth attempt, I have now given up.

For a company the size of ASOS, this level of customer service is incredibly frustrating. The product quality was poor, but the complete lack of follow-up or accountability has been even more disappointing.

I hope someone at ASOS can review this and finally resolve the issue.

What have ASOS done to their customer service... They infuriate you with AI that sends you round in loops and then I had 2 separate 'live' people be rude and completely unhelpful and lie to me about a mistake ASOS made sending to the wrong address. Telling me I should simply go collect it from another part of the country. They said it's policy to send it bac to the default delivery address.. but they didn't! They sent it to an old billing address that I now cannot get to.

Essentially robbed me of £65
It's a shame, I used to buy from here somewhat often. 
To boil it down; I made a return but they wiped the items status as 'returned' from my account/app.
I had the return label they generated and proof of postage / delivery etc. 

When going through support I found out that they've completely gotten rid of their customer support and left a useless chatbot in place... I'm still in disbelief, (what the f are they thinking??)
This chat bot obviously can't help me with processing a refund. It still tries to forward me onto a human, but there's no one on the other end. 

I'm never buying here again.

Asos stole my money. Back in November I purchased some Adidas shorts from Asos. I never received the shorts, I received some random socks instead. I asked Asos rep what to do, they told me to return the socks to Adidas and stick a piece of paper in the parcel explaining what happened (Already something I shouldn't have to spend time doing, as this is not my error, they should have contacted their brand partner to look into/solve issue!). Anyways I did what Asos rep told me, then received an email from Adidas asking what the socks are about (as if I hadn't explained in the piece of paper). Despite me explaining clearly they have totally lost trace and me as the customer is now having to carry the consequences of this unsuccessful brand partnership and appalling logistical operations!

Months down the line, I haven't got the shorts or a refund for the shorts!!! Asos now tells me unless I have the postage receipt from the socks they cannot help me - despite Adidas acknowledging they received the socks in an email, with order number and everything!!!! I obviously did not keep safe my postage receipt months after I got confirmation of its safe arrival! 

I should also mention both customer service departments are incredibly incompetent, sending me incoherent responses that aren't relevant to what I'm saying, Asos says "We need proof of postage so we can check if we received it in our warehouse" to me saying "You told me to send it to Adidas, so you won't receive it in your warehouse!", while Adidas sends me an email advising what to with the customer (me!) and taking to me like i'm the Asos rep. 
And what is to happen to my £49 while neither company can get themselves in gear???? Absolutely appalling
The customer service of Asos is the worst of all! They are not helping and they wont listen. Even if i aks for an email adress to file a complaint they wont give it. Wow really ridiculous.

Ordered 16 pair of pants only 12 arrived. Started a chat with customer service, where they apologized and arrange to send me the 4 missing pants. After 20 minutes I receive a confirmation for refund. Started a new chat where all items all the sudden were out of stock. They weren't willing to help and kept blaming everything on human errors. The customer service also felt AI generated due to every sentence ending with: I'm here to support you, can I do anything else.

This is the second time I’ve had the worst online shopping experience with ASOS. They obviously have technical problems. The first time they sent me the wrong sizes, and the second time the entire package was delivered incorrectly. Despite contacting customer support and asking them not to charge me for their mistakes, I had to pay the full shipping and return costs both times.

Nearly €7 for nothing – just to pay for their errors. I’m deleting the app and I never want to hear about them again.

I used to order from ASOS all the time. Never had any issues whatsoever even with returns. They are using a delivery service called GOFO which is a total scam company. I placed an order I was home all day I received an email that said my package was delivered My door will never ring I went outside there was no package anywhere. I contacted ASOS they told me I need to take it up with the delivery service because they have confirmation that my package was delivered It was a photo of the front of my house with my address and a picture of the package well let me tell you something We were outside all day nobody ever came. It's very easy to get a photo of an address and place a picture through AI. It didn't just happen once it happened twice. Who delivers a package and doesn't bring it to the door. Every other company does that's because the package never showed up. You're afraid of me the delivery service told me to take it up with ASOS and vice versa. So I'm out $125. I will never order from this company again...

I’m very disappointed with my recent experience with ASOS customer service.

I have been a loyal ASOS customer for many years and have placed many orders with them. Because of this, I was really surprised by how my situation was handled. I missed the return window because I didn’t realise that the return policy had changed from 30 days to 14 days. Since ASOS had a longer return period before, this change unfortunately caught me by surprise.

I contacted customer service hoping that a small one-time exception could be made, especially as a long-time customer. However, they refused to help or offer any flexibility. What makes this even more frustrating is that I’ve seen reviews here from other customers who said they were helped with returns even after the return period had passed.

I completely understand that companies have policies, but good customer service should also consider loyal customers and individual situations. This experience has honestly made me reconsider ordering from ASOS in the future.

I hope ASOS can review my case again and show the kind of customer care that many people expect from such a large company.

Really bad delivery service. The company lies and says they tried to deliver but they did not. Automatically returned to asos and still waiting for a refund. First and last time I will order from this joke of a company.

Mrs ordered a lot of items from Asos having used them many times before. Long story short, after a big order there was £1,200 to return whilst the rest were being kept! Items went into the returns locker and confirmation received including the fact that a day or two later they had received the return! Money didnt come back, painful process of contacting them only for Asos to say they didnt receive the items? Having proved the return was complete including their acceptance notice and warehouse confirming the return, they refused the refund. It was an easy case through the bank who refunded and collected the money immediately. Asos had confirmed the return and signed by the warehouse, so the bank easily used this and case closed. Bottom line, after years of being a customer, never again! They are dishonest, scammers, liars, simple as that! Never again you dishonest untrustworthy company! Shame on you!

I received two packages and wanted to return them. I contacted customer service and sent them back, but after 15 days there was still no refund. I reached out again but got no resolution.

After a full month, I contacted them once more and still hadn’t received my money back as promised.

Very disappointing experience. The refund process seems completely disorganized, and I never received the money I was told I would get.

Also it takes 20+ minutes for them to connect you to a human agent…

If I could I’d rether give it non star!
 The worst customer service and after-sales support! They don't solve any problems at all! I didn't receive the goods. The courier company sent an email saying it was automatically returned to ASOS, but I still haven't received a refund after a month! No one from after-sales has solved the problem.

Don’t buy at ASOS. My order came 5 days later than planned, meaning i could not take it with me on vacation. Then the costumer service expected me to register the return during that period so it met the 14 day registration period. i could not try the clothes since i was away. so how do they expect me to register a return? well, they say, i should have done it just to comply with the rule even i later decided to keep the items. this makes no sense at all. they did not offer me a solution. the worst costumer service i have ever had in my life. i spent 1 h chatting with them for nothing.
Bad quality. ASOS is not what it used to be 5 years ago.

Asos always fails to deliver next day yet charges £5.95 for it. They’ve switched from evri to Royal Mail meaning they can’t be delivering past certain hours either. This is the 2nd time my order hasn’t arrived on time, they’ve done what every other business has done and replaced human contact with AI so I’m constantly waiting for an agent and unable to get my refund. Delivery service and customer service has not improved and not up to standards.
Tried to return an item that didn't fit and was told it was reject as it was worn! It is impossible to challenge this was basically told, decision is final. This is disgusting!

I was a loyal customer for years always paid on time and most of the time so happy with my purchases. On multiple occasions I was missing items or had things damaged don’t know if that was from asos or delivery drivers always sent photos of how parcels would arrive sometimes in a box that was barely holding together I have heard this happen to other people aswell. Then with no reason given had my account blocked like I’ve done something wrong when I haven’t clearly and now can’t use asos have tried to ask why and to sort this and no help what so ever so am currently seeking legal advice as I had over £100 in vouchers on there I never got to use that they have no right to keep from me!! I have heard so much negativity about asos over the last couple of years gone down hill etc. I just want my account reopened and an apology or my vouchers of the money sent to me and be done with the company as it’s beyond wrong!!!
I have really bad experience with the customer service chat(?) I have problems with my refund and spent almost 2 hours just for them to ended my chat, such a shame

Do not buy from Asos if you are a VOEC member country!!! They forget the VOEC number and you end up having to pay VAT/taxes twice. First to asos and then to local customs. Asos is trying to blame this on fedex and customs, but they are forced to declare the package as long as VOEC number is missing. Meaning asos has charged me for meaningless VAT, im charged twice!! And they refuse to take accountability and refund me

I’m genuinely shocked at the negativity. I don’t often come on here to leave reviews, but I ordered some shoes by mistake and the refund was processed so quickly, including postage. Very efficient and smooth customer service. I’ve not had any delivery issues either, always on time. Thank you ASOS 😊

ASOS has some beautiful clothes and I’ve ordered from them many times, but the sizing is completely inconsistent. I usually wear a size 12 and that’s what my measurements are, yet when I order a 12 it can arrive either far too small or far too big. I’ve even tried ordering a 14 to compensate, but again it’s either too tight or way too loose. There seems to be no consistency at all.

Because of this, I’ve had to return multiple items, and now I’m being penalised for it. I’m being charged for returns and my delivery times have been extended. My most recent order was placed on Monday with the promise “order within the next 4 hours for next day delivery,” but now it’s taking 7 days. The items I ordered for my birthday will arrive after my birthday, so they’ll just be sent straight back.

It’s really disappointing because the clothes themselves are lovely, but the unreliable sizing and new return policies make it very difficult to shop with confidence. Sadly, I’ll be closing my account after this experience. Not that it will matter to a company this size, but ASOS really needs to do better with their sizing consistency.

Terrible customer service and misleading website. 2 product pages exactly the same but different prices. I understand that ASOS policy generally requires returns to be sent from the original country of delivery. However, when I asked if I could generate a return from Hong Kong instead (I live on the border), your team told me they could and sent a ReBOUND confirmation email.

That email clearly stated:
1. "Present your QR code in Circle K stores"
2. "See email attachment for QR Code"
3. "Payment Amount: FREE"

However, no QR code was provided in the email, only a barcode.

When I queried this, I was told the barcode was the "only option available." When I asked how it should be used and where it could be scanned, I was told I would need to "figure it out" myself.

I travelled to Hong Kong then next day with the parcel, taking unpaid leave from work because this was expected to be a quick return drop-off. Instead, I visited multiple Circle K stores, all of which confirmed they require a QR code and could not process the barcode provided.

I then spent over 2.5 hours on live chat being transferred between multiple agents. During that time:
• I had to repeat the entire situation several times from the beginning.
• I was sent additional barcodes without explanation.
• I was told to try Kerry Locker, but no clear location guidance was provided.
• I was told to attach a QR code to the parcel, despite not being given one.
• I was later informed that a QR code cannot be generated.
• I was ultimately told the only option was to return via the Post Office at my own expense.

The tone throughout was dismissive rather than solution-focused. Questions were often answered vaguely, and I was repeatedly passed to another agent instead of being given clear guidance.

Finally, I was placed on hold for a manager and waited approximately 40 minutes with no response. The only eventual reply was that they couldn't help me but if I wanted to escalate, I could and that I would receive an email.

If returning from Hong Kong was not genuinely possible, the return should never have been created in the first place. The written instructions referenced a QR code that was never provided, stated "Payment Amount: FREE" and yet the only practical option offered requires significant additional cost.

Throughout the 2.5 hours on live chat I received contradictory guidance and unusable return documentation, unhelpful handling of the situation, and unnecessary time and expense caused by poor customer service.

I have used ASOS for years without issue and have previously returned items from Hong Kong without any problems. This experience, however, has been so poorly handled that I will not be using ASOS again.

Extremely disappointing.

Update: it’s been 2 weeks and I haven’t had an email from the escalation team about the complaint I made via live chat, and I still have no way to return the parcel.

After speaking to customer service, I was told the trousers were “heavily worn and had an odour,” which is completely untrue. It’s impossible for me to have worn trousers that were excessively long, and there was no odour or signs of wear whatsoever when I returned them.

After three months of trying to resolve a simple issue with Debenhams (owned by Boohoo Group), I am still no further forward. I have already posted a detailed review on Debenhams’ Trustpilot page and as expected, the only response I received was the usual copy‑and‑paste line telling me to “contact customer service”. That would be fine if customer service actually responded, but they either take more than three weeks at a time to reply or give completely contradictory information when they finally do.

For context, my order arrived damaged in December. I cancelled the replacement immediately, yet Debenhams sent it anyway. They then contested the chargeback with my bank while ignoring my cancellation and multiple messages. When I finally reached the Escalations Team, I was told I needed to close the charge back, even though doing so would remove my protection and the dispute was only happening because Debenhams ignored me in the first place. I replied immediately and since then I’ve had 14 days of silence. This has now been going on for three months.

I’m posting this here because Boohoo Group own Debenhams, and at this point I need someone at a senior level to actually take responsibility instead of passing the buck back to the same customer service channels that have already failed repeatedly. I fully expect the standard “please contact customer service” reply to this review too, but after three months of delays, contradictions and silence, that simply isn’t good enough.

If I could offer advice based on my experience, it would be to avoid shopping with either Debenhams or Boohoo Group altogether. The customer service is shambolic.

The goods arrived at their destination and an email was received to acknowledge this, but to try and get a refund from Debenhams was a complete nightmare.  Despite me having a Debenhams order number they said they couldn't find it on their system despite me sending numerous emails with photographic images of said item, tracking number and order number.  Sadly email is the only way to contact Debenhams, as there is no telephone number which is annoying in itself.  I also feel alot of the time it's a robot that you are trying to get your message across to which is akin to banging your head against a brick wall, and so utterly frustrating. Because of this horrendous experience I shall NEVER ever buy anything from Debenhams again which is such a shame as they do have some lovely things.  Please Debenhams if you can be bothered to read this review get your act together and listen to what customers are saying and get yourselves organised to provide good customer service.  If big companies like M and S and John Lewis can achieve it so can you.

I placed an order on January 3rd, but it didn’t arrive until February 14th. The same day the delivery partner dropped it off at my door, I attempted to return the item. However, I was told that I was outside of the 28-day return window.

I explained that this was unacceptable, considering the delay was caused by the shipping process and completely out of my control. I asked to speak with several managers, but each time I was told, “Unfortunately, you are outside of the time frame.”

Only after I mentioned that I would report the situation to the BBB did the issue suddenly get resolved and a refund was issued.

In my opinion, it should not have required that level of escalation for a reasonable request. If an item takes over six weeks to be delivered, it’s not fair to hold the customer responsible for a return window that effectively expired before the package even arrived. The delay was due to BoohooMAN’s chosen third-party delivery partners, not the customer.

Refund turned into expiring gift cards – £104.69 lost

I’ve been a customer of Boohoo for years but this experience has been extremely disappointing.

I returned items and my refunds were issued as gift cards without me clearly realising, each with a 3-month expiry. I have now been told that two of these expired and £104.69 of my refund has been lost.

Breakdown Boohoo sent me:
	•	£13.90 – expired
	•	£90.79 – expired
	•	£3.72 – still active
	•	£35.21 – already spent

Customer service simply told me expired gift cards cannot be reinstated, meaning over £100 of my own refund has disappeared.

I do not believe this is fair or transparent. Refunds should normally go back to the original payment method, and a 3-month expiry on refund vouchers is extremely short.

I have asked Boohoo to either reinstate the expired vouchers or refund £104.69, but so far they have refused.

I hope Boohoo reviews this and resolves the issue because at the moment this feels very unfair to customers.

I was sent a faulty item and had already paid £6.99 for next day delivery, only to receive something unusable. I was then expected to pay to return the faulty item myself, which is unacceptable. I contacted about returning the damaged item on 05 February 2026 I requested a prepaid returns label, After generic replies and multiple threads I was ultimately forced to open a dispute with PayPal to get a refund for the item. Only then to have to pay for tracked delivery to return the item in order to get my refund. 

I  contacted Boohoo on  07 March 26 requesting reimbursement for both the delivery (£6.99) and the £5.40 return postage. Despite providing proof, I received no proper response. After waiting over a week, I was sent a generic reply stating there were “no return charges”, which completely ignored the issue.

Communication has been extremely poor, with responses coming from multiple email threads, which only adds confusion and makes the situation harder to resolve. The actual problem has still not been properly addressed.

I have now had to escalate further through my bank to recover the return postage. This entire process has taken weeks (now 20 March) and has been unnecessarily time-consuming and stressful.

I have now also reported this matter to Citizens Advice so it can be passed to Trading Standards.

Customers should not have to chase, repeat themselves, or quote the Consumer Rights Act 2015 just to be treated fairly.

If you are in a similar situation, do not accept this – escalate through PayPal, your bank, or Citizens Advice.

I have also reported this issue to the Competition and Markets Authority due to concerns about how faulty returns are being handled. 

The more customers who report this type of behaviour, the more pressure there is on companies to act responsibly and follow consumer law.

This is going to be long but in summary: 

The quality of their clothes is much worse than any other fast fashion retailer and their sizing is inconsistent, so you will inevitably have to return items... and aside from having issues with refunding (as seen in many other reviews) you're at a dead end if you require any sort of assistance because their customer service is non-existent. It takes days to reach a human but the chat gets reset if there is no response in a short amount of time and they take days to respond, so you're back to talking to AI, and... repeat the cycle. In the end, I had to claim my refund after disputing and months of investigation through my bank.

AVOID THIS "BUSINESS" AT ALL COSTS.

Now, the detailed review, for anyone interested...

I tried to return an order and emailed pre-emptively as I wanted to make sure I was doing it right since I had two parcels. They never responded. I emailed again. No response. 

I decided to just go ahead with the return before the return window closed and at the Post Office, I found out I was right about my concerns and could not return two parcels in one return. I tried to cancel the return and redo it by splitting it into two returns or something, but it wouldn't allow me. It had to be cancelled on their end. 

I emailed immediately with the issue and waited. Lo and behold, I received no response. I still had not received a response to my original emails from weeks ago as well.

I went onto their site and found that there was a WhatsApp contact avenue - but when clicked on, it would send me to an Error 404 page (i.e. this page does not exist.) 

I then messaged them on Instagram. I was getting sent round in circles by the automated response and after I asked multiple times for a human, I was put in a queue. I was warned that wait times are long. I waited. They did not respond. 

Then, as a last resort, I downloaded Facebook and its messenger and contacted them through there. A day later, I FINALLY received a response. 

But guess what? They said they couldn't help me because the return window had closed and didn't care that I'd been trying to get help and contact them for over three weeks now. 

This was when I finally received a response from Instagram. I responded and, this time they said they would allow me to return despite the return window closing. I did as I was asked and explained the situation, as well as send screenshots of my multiple ignored emails as asked, and I waited. After getting no response, I messaged again... and got an automated response. The chat had been reset. I was no longer talking or responding to a human. 

This started a long cycle of being stuck between automated responses, waiting for a human, getting a reply and responding, then being sent back to AI and getting a different support agent and re-explaining, and the chat being reset after no response.... you get the idea.

I had enough and disputed through my bank. I sent all my emails, my Instagram chat, my Facebook Messenger chat, and eventually, after a month of waiting, got my refund.

But it didn't end there.

This was when I finally got my first response via email. So their email IS active. They just don't respond. I suspect they only did so because I disputed the order through my bank. They asked for my order number and reason for return so that they could generate a new return QR code. I responded. This is when I found out that Boohoo had disputed my claim.

They did not respond to my response email. Their dispute was not successful. I kept my money. But I had gone through months of back and forth with them and my bank.

Just avoid them. Or take the risk, but if you have any sort of issue, rest assured you will not be getting any sort of help from them.

I am writing this review after weeks of going back and forth with River Island about a missing order and being left completely out of pocket.

I placed order for around £276 which was sent via DPD. The tracking shows it as “delivered” on 13 November 2025 at 14:52, but I never received this parcel. No parcel at my door, no parcel in a safe place, no card, nothing with my neighbours – the parcel simply did not arrive.

I contacted River Island customer service straight away and followed every step they asked of me. I filled in their non-receipt declaration form and cooperated fully with their “investigation”. I was initially told that a refund request had been submitted and that it would be reviewed by their Customer Resolutions Team.

After all of this, I then received an email saying that they had “not identified anything that would explain why I have not received my order” and that they were therefore refusing to refund me. They also stated that they would no longer respond about this issue unless I had “new information” and suggested I seek independent legal advice if I wasn’t satisfied. This is despite the fact that I have clearly reported that I haven’t had the goods I paid for.

So I am now in a situation where:
	•	River Island has my £276
	•	I do not have my order
	•	They have effectively closed the case and pushed me towards legal action rather than resolving the problem

This feels incredibly unfair and completely at odds with basic consumer rights – if an item is not received, the retailer should deal with the courier and ensure the customer is either sent a replacement or refunded. Instead, I’ve been made to feel as though I’m not believed, even though I have done everything they asked.

The most frustrating part is the tone of the final response: it was cold, dismissive and gave the impression that they were more interested in protecting themselves than in helping a loyal customer who is simply asking for either the goods they paid for or their money back.

I am now having to explore this further with Citizens Advice / Trading Standards and my payment provider, which is stressful and time-consuming – all over an order that never turned up.

I’m leaving this review so other customers are aware that if something goes wrong with delivery, you may find yourself stuck between the courier and River Island, with nobody taking real responsibility. At the moment, I would not feel confident ordering from River Island again, knowing that if a parcel goes missing, the default response seems to be to deny any responsibility and refuse a refund.

I hope River Island reconsiders how they handle lost parcel claims, reviews my case properly, and does the right thing by refunding me for the goods I never received.

This was, without any doubt, one of the worst customer service experiences I have ever had. I came to this store to return a dress I bought the previous weekend, with my receipt and full proof of payment. What should have been a simple return became an unacceptable and deeply disrespectful situation entirely caused by the behaviour of the staff.

From the very beginning, the first staff member’s attitude was already unprofessional and unpleasant. Before I even had the chance to explain anything, she spoke extremely quickly and with visible impatience, making it very hard for me to understand her. When I told her that I couldn’t follow what she was saying, her behaviour immediately became even more rude and hostile.

She refused to listen, constantly interrupted me, and insisted there was a problem with my payment simply because the card number did not match (something completely normal when paying with a smartwatch).

When she started going through my Google Wallet on my phone, I clearly told her that I did not have to explain the details of my personal cards. She insisted that I did have to explain myself simply because the numbers didn’t match. This was intrusive, inappropriate, and absolutely unacceptable. She had no right to demand personal information from my device when I had already provided valid proof of purchase.

A second staff member later joined, without greeting me, without asking what had happened. Instead, she approached the situation with the same hostile and confrontational attitude. She also made completely unfounded assumptions, including an accusation related to her accent (something both absurd and deeply offensive, especially considering that my own accent is very obvious, and I am clearly not from the UK). Accusing me of reacting to someone’s accent, when mine is extremely noticeable, was shocking and completely unjustified.

Throughout all of this, I remained calm and cooperative. I even offered my bank statement to confirm the payment, yet the hostility continued. Only one staff member behaved professionally and genuinely tried to assist.

The worst part happened when I was leaving. The first staff member was waiting by the entrance, and as I walked out, she laughed directly in my face saying with ironic tone: "Have a FANTASTIC day" or something silly like that, very loud. I just walked out loughing. I told her ‘good luck’ as I laughed, because the Christmas season coming up in the next few weeks, if she is already behaving like this now, I can’t imagine how she will be then, whole situation was so miserable, and it is even more worst when I heard her loudly repeating my laugh inside the store. This behaviour is disgraceful, childish, and absolutely intolerable. No customer should ever be mocked, humiliated, or treated with such disrespect.

I filed a formal complaint with Customer Services immediately after this incident, and I fully expect store management and the business owners to address this seriously. What happened was not “poor service”, it was completely unacceptable conduct that shows a total lack of respect for customers.

I hope this review helps others understand what I experienced and encourages the business to urgently improve its customer service standards.

I am absolutely furious with the appalling experience I’ve had with River Island’s online service. I’ve shopped in-store many times and always thought highly of the brand — but this first attempt to order online has completely destroyed that trust.

When I reached out to customer service, I was told there was “nothing that could be done” — that I’d have to wait until the item arrived, only to return it again for a refund. That’s absurd. For a company of your size, it’s shocking that you can’t process a simple cancellation seconds after an order is made.
Still, I followed your instructions, returned the item as soon as it arrived, and was told I’d get my full refund — and that returns were free. What followed was weeks of back-and-forth with your team, endless waiting, and constant frustration. It took nearly a month to get my refund back, only to discover you’d deducted £3 for no reason.
Let me be clear: it’s not about the £3 — it’s about the complete lack of honesty, professionalism, and respect for your customers. You told me returns were free, yet you still took money off and made me chase you for weeks to get what was rightfully mine. This is unacceptable, and frankly, disgraceful customer service.
This whole experience has been a joke. My daughter is still waiting for her wellies, and I’m left disgusted with how poorly River Island has handled the situation. I feel let down, disrespected, and absolutely fed up.
I expect this issue to be resolved immediately — including the return of the missing £3 — and a proper apology for the unacceptable treatment I’ve received.

I'm sadly not surprised that River Island is closing stores and likely to go in to administration. Their clothing is hugely over priced & has been for decades. You would expect superior quality for the prices they charge, but no, the quality of the material, sewing/finish & fit is poor. There is also no quality control happening. I bought some jeans in the sale last year, loved the first pair, so ordered another. When I received the second pair, they were a different length despite supposed to be the same as the first pair I ordered. I really wanted another pair, so ordered another 3 pairs in hope 1 would be right-EVERY pair was a different length & the fit was different to the first pair I bought too, despite supposed to be the exact same item. I had to send them all back. You should be able to order the exact same item & receive another item that measures the exact same, but no not at River Island. Also measurements are very big at River Island. I'm normally a size 6, but even their size 4 jeans are a little too big in the waist & they don't do every style in a size 4 (short) so it limits my choice. The leg lengths are also longer than every other shop! In all other stores a short inside leg is 28 inches, standerd is 30 inches & long is 32 inches. At River Island a short is 30/31 inches, standard 32/33 inches & long 34/35 inches, so if you are 5'1 like me then everything is too long at this shop. I've even ordered petite jeans that were longer in length than a short in their normal range! The jeans also go REALLY big & baggy once you've washed them, so they never fit as good after washing as when you first buy them & wear them. I wash them inside out, never wash above 30 degrees, use a short cycle, I've tried different detergents, don't use fabric conditioner, even tried washing just in water with no detergent, I air dry them, but it makes no difference. The jeans lose their shape & end up too big & baggy & hanging off me as soon as they've been washed and they've had just one wear. This happens with different styles. I wish they would go back to making jeans without elastane, like they used to in the 90's. Jeans would slightly relax after a few wears, but would never go big & baggy after 1 wear when they were made WITHOUT elastane back then. They were much better without elastane.

I placed an order with Nasty Gal on 22 July 2025, selecting their UK Super Saver Delivery option (5–7 working days). As of early August, my parcel has still not arrived, and I have received no meaningful support.

Despite multiple attempts to contact customer service, I have only encountered an automated AI chatbot, with no access to a real person. Most concerningly, I have now been informed that my order number and email address are “not recognised” — this, despite the fact that Nasty Gal have not only emailed me marketing content, but have also successfully taken the payment from my account.

In other words, my order appears to “not exist” in their system, yet the funds were withdrawn without issue. The contradiction is staggering.

After reading recent reviews on Trustpilot, it is evident that I am not alone. Many other customers report unfulfilled orders, being ignored by customer service, and a total lack of transparency or accountability.

This experience has raised serious concerns about whether the company’s systems have been compromised, or whether they are simply failing to provide the most basic standard of customer care.

I do not typically leave reviews, but I feel compelled to share my experience, as there appears to be no other effective method of communication with this company.

So absurd has this experience become that I have even considered creating a TikTok video documenting the situation — not for entertainment, but because it seems to be the only way to get a response or warn other potential customers of the risk.

To summarise:
	•	My order has not arrived
	•	My payment has been taken
	•	I have received no assistance
	•	I have not been able to speak with a human representative
	•	My order “doesn’t exist”, according to their system

This has been, without exaggeration, the worst customer service experience I have ever encountered. If I could give zero stars, I absolutely would.

I strongly advise others to reconsider ordering from Nasty Gal until these issues are publicly acknowledged and resolved.

If I could give less than 1 star I would! Please DO NOT ORDER!! I wish someone would have told me sooner! I ordered a denim jacket and shorts for a concert and my total was $300. I reached out to the customer service team to let them know that the items were defective and I got an answer within 3 days. They told me that they sent me a return label to return the items, but the label didn’t even work. I still had to go through the returns portal on the app and start a new return to even get a label. I chose to go with the online label. I shipped the items back to them at the post office. First the items sat for days with no tracking updates. I contacted USPS to ask for an update and they let me know that the items would be shipped differently due to the return label and that it was waiting to be picked up by a return shipping carrier. At this point it had been 6 days since I dropped it off, so I was shocked to hear that it needed to be picked up by UPS instead of just being shipped out like I expected. The item has been “in transit” for over two weeks and each time I check the tracking, it says that it’s ready for pickup. I’m not sure why it’s being picked up, or what strange return labels they use, but it is not what you’d expect and it will take forever for the items to get back to them as it will be bouncing between USPS and UPS. At this point, my return window will have closed by the time it finally (if ever) arrives back to them. I believe this is calculated on their part as a way to not give customers a refund. The return shipping label they give you will take so long that they’ll then be able to say that it was out of the return date and they can’t refund you. This company is a SCAM and is not worth it. At this point, I will be disputing the charge with my bank to hopefully get my money back. Please, please, please, don’t order anything from them! I wish someone would’ve told me sooner!!

This is so bad. I returned more clothes then I received money for. Absolute bollocs. You own me £19 pounds. Is this is how you scum people for money now? The clothes are terrible quality. Great item and fast standard delivery

Long winded returns process, they charge £2.95 to return. Can’t find order to return on the website even though I put in order number. Doesn’t specify price on the order confirmation email to easily keep track of refund amount. I’d be thinking a lot before ordering online from them again.

The reviews of products on their website don't seem honest. I bought a few tee shirts on line and the quality is awful (basically landfill) but they had over 4 star reviews. I am unable to leave a reviews myself, very odd and untrustworthy.

I bought a lovely soft pink cardigan in November. I have worn the cardigan no more than 6 times and each time have carefully hand washed it. Disappointed to see its become really bobbly and the wool hard. Took it back to the Harlow branch and was told by a very abrupt assistant that it’s wear and tear (after 6 wears!!!) and nothing they could do. Wouldn’t listen to me explain and just cut me dead. I complained to customer services who offered me a £10 discount off my next purchase. Online Returns take forever to process even when returning to store. Puts me off ordering online from this company when other stores process much more efficiently.
Never had a problem with New Look delivery times or returns. Returned an item to store on Mon 23rd Feb and just had message from PayPal  Wed 25th Feb refund is in account.

I also have to say there was nothing wrong with the items I returned, they were just too big for me.  All items I received were delivered next day and on time by Evri and all items were the correct colour and size I ordered.

You just literally lost a customer - I will never buy from New Look ever again. To be frank, you offer one of the worst, unaccommodating and very inflexible customer services in the industry. And a note to Phoebe from Customer Care - absolutely NO thanks to you for your rigidity in servicing and solving valid return complaints and requests from your customer. No attempts to offer goodwill just to retain customers (which is so much cheaper than customer acquisition btw - perhaps something your customer care team does NOT know about). First and last time to buy from New Look it is! Never Again - please avoid at all costs, not worth your stress.

Having bought some good jogger bottoms (cuffed) a few years ago, I bought 2 more pairs recently. The quality and size has changed SO much, it's rediculous! I had already bought a pair recently my previous size, which were so enormous and SO long, it was unbelievable. I angrily gave them to my husband. . Also, the material used was so different. The quality is really poor and the sizes are unbelievably variable and have no connection whatsoever to the sizes referred to on their website!! It is really bad. What a total waste of time and energy, especially with the very awkward returns policy.  Attempting to discuss it all with customer services was a joke until I had tried about 6 times.  I will NEVER buy from New Look again.   Lisa Robinson
Im so disappointed Ihavent received my order

Whilst visiting Manchester for a couple days, on Wednesday afternoon I went into Manchester Arndale’s store. The store itself was lovely, both layout and clothing. Customer service however? Vile. I bought I pair of jeans and was served by a girl called Emer. I’ve never been served by someone so pushy. A few years ago I did actually used to work for a New Look store. So I know how on it the company is about email marketing. But I felt very uncomfortable. I was asked if I was a new look member and I replied no. I was then told if I signed up I would’ve got 15% off my denim. To which I then replied no thank you I’m okay. I wasn’t fussed about a discount. If I don’t want to be a company’s member I don’t have to be. Customers are well within their rights to say no to things and it seems a lot of choice is being taken away from the shopper experience. The response I got back was a very unnecessary, sarcastic response of “oh you don’t want your 15%? Ok that’s fine then”. I paid for my item and then came the whole email receipt rigmarole. I was asked for an email for my receipt to which I asked if it was possible to just have a physical paper one. The girl serving me tried to make out we needed an email receipt in order to be given a physical one. Now I know this isn’t the case as like I’ve previously stated, I worked within the company all but about 4 years ago. I again politely re-iterated I wanted the paper receipt. I was met with yet again another rude and sarcastic response of “well if you lose the receipt there’s no refund or exchanges, thanks bye”. Very unprofessional. 

I’m aware this is their job to ask these questions as I’ve been in their shoes before. But there’s no need to be so rude and so pushy with it. The company is having many of their stores shut because people won’t shop there anymore due to  being met with so many questions and just such rudeness.

Went into the Tunbridge wells branch ( Northfarm) False advertising, none of it makes any kind of sense ( and I’m in retail management) was fobbed off by staff and then the Manager who just waffled on not knowing what she was talking about. This is what happens when kids are trying to be managers. Because it’s cheaper for companies. Then trying to get in touch with head office well that’s a shambles also. Never again and I’d urge people to check prices in Newlook scammers

I've been shopping at New Look all my life and am still able to find flattering and age appropriate fashionable styles of decent quality at good prices.  Some of the reviews here are worrying but I have never had a problem with online orders and the staff at my local store in East Grinstead are always smart, welcoming and helpful.
4 days ago you emailed me to say you have received my return. Still waiting for my refund!. How long is it going to take?? Order number- HM811867694.. just not good enough!

I ordered some shoes from them online and collected in store. Went to pick up the item and it was a big soft package, certainly not shoes. Couldn't return them in store, did the whole online return over 2 weeks ago. Inpost tracking says delivered to their warehouse, new look tracking not updated. No customer service to talk to I just have to hope and wait for an email reply eventually. I'll be claiming back through klarna if I get to 21 days with no response. They used to be good! I used to order items internationally living in South Korea and had no issues now they can't even get my order or refund right...

Absolutely atrocious service.

I returned an online order and was given an incorrect refund amount. Despite providing the order confirmation and my bank statement showing the exact amount charged, I have been lied to about pricing and sent conflicting explanations that do not match what I actually paid.

I’ve spent over 30 minutes in store and gone back and forth via multiple emails, only to be left with an outstanding refund and no proper explanation. A formal complaint was raised, then closed without resolution, and I was even sent a feedback request as if the issue had been resolved — it hasn’t.

At this point, I’ve been left with no option but to raise a chargeback through my bank to recover the money I’m owed.

I have been a loyal customer for many years, but after this experience, I will never shop here again. Completely unacceptable handling of a straightforward refund and shocking customer service.

Paid for return of an item of poor quality, to be collected by Evri.  I even selected the date for collection. Evri did not turn up on the day and their tracking showed I was booked in but waiting for the item from me.  I contacted New Look, who advised me that I could take the item to a drop off point, with no offer of contacting  Evri to resolve the situation, instead just continue to give advice of how to book a collection.  When I finally told the "teammate", I would keep the item for return after becoming fraudstrated, he informed me the collection fee was non refundable as it was paid to Evri.  No attempt again to find a solution, so Evri has money for doing nothing. Then I get a message from New Look saying they would be closing my query, even though I was still talking to their teammate.  An absolute shambles of a customer service.

Been waiting over a month for a refund.  No one to talk to and only bots that reply and can't understand situation.
Have proof of return but they don't refund me!

Impossible to contact. Do not sign up for the club unless you want a ridiculous amount of emails. Endless emails without the option to unsubscribe on the email. Tells you to contact them online which says they will contact you in 5 days. I have already emailed to say remove my email over a week ago and still getting so many emails!!! Stop emailing me!!

They make it impossible to contact anyone. Been trying to cancel my order and seem unable to do this. Despite trying within 20 mins of ordering and leaving a message as they won’t reply until 3-5 days. What good is that??

Avoid like the plague.

I purchased a gift from new look before xmas and ever since have been bombarded with marketing emails.
There is no way to remove yourself from these in the email itself (illegal) and you can only untick 2 items on their website yet the emails continue to flood through.
Contacted them, asked to be removed, took a few days of back and forth and was promised they would stop and i was removed.
I then recieved a further email to say i was removed but my details would then stay on their system for another 12 months (wtf?). Unless, I replied back and ticked yet another option to have my details removed, to which I did.
fast forward 2 days, guess what, yes yet another marketing email.

I'm genuinely amazed of the malpractice this company does for marketing, it will just push away genuine customers. 
I can no longer log in yet they continue to bombard me with spam.
I have now reported them to the ICO and I advise all other customers to do the same. They will receive fine upon fine until they stop.

This is the 2nd time I've had to chase refund. I returned items and have waited way past the 14 days which they state they will refund. There's nobody available to speak to, all automated messages. 
I won't be shopping with NL again, I used to love this brand and buy most of my clothes from here but I'll go elsewhere now.

I don’t even want to give one star for ‘New Look,  but I  have to! New Look’s return policy is an absolute shambles! I paid for 2 orders using Klarna, so I did as instructed and logged the online returns, both separately from two orders, and received the QR codes by email. My husband who works in town, took the parcels to my New Look store on my behalf, to return, and showed them the QR codes! Staff then said he would need my debit card to do return. Why, if it’s all in the QR code from online return and payment would be going back to my Klarna account. Staff have no idea what they are saying or doing. He arrived back with the parcels yesterday! Today again, along with my debit card, went back to the New Look store to return them, and today, their excuse is, that I would need a bar code from Klarna (supposedly sent at time of order) I knew nothing about this, and it was not mentioned online nor on Klarna! No return again! Parcels are back home tonight, so with no other choice, I tried to do the returns by post, but couldn’t get them registered through ‘My orders’ on New look website, as they had already been logged as ‘Returns’ .. no option to change process to accommodate my trying to return these items, through post, that store refused to take back twice!! Now tonight online, in my online account, it is telling me, it is too late to return ‘said’ orders as they are out of their time?? Unbelievable. I have spent thousands of pounds with ‘New look, both in store and online, but that is me finished. I will never shop with them again. Simply isn’t worth the bother and hassle, we have to go through, to try and make any returns. As of now, I have 2 bags of clothing here, which weren’t suitable due to bad sizing (new looks) own fault, that are of no use to me and don’t fit! Many pounds worth of orders, in my home, that I can’t return, due to one issue after another, either with the staff, who have no clue what they are doing, and actually admitted it, and no further way to return on online website. Absolute assault course, trying to return items. Why should I have to pay £2.99 for each and every return by post, when they could all go in one parcel?? I won’t be putting myself through this ever again. You need to train your staff on how to do returns from various payment sources, else they will all be out of jobs and new look won’t exist anymore? You have just lost another long term customer, and I wouldn’t recommend New Look to anyone now, due to the ridiculous returns process. Don’t shop at New Look, in case the clothing doesn’t fit! You will be tearing your hair out and still won’t be able to return and get your money back, regardless how many ways you try to go about it!

New Look seems to have gone really downhill. The quality of their clothing is poor. Not only does the material feel cheap but it is visibly cheap and the cuts and styles are very poor these days. On top of this, they make it impossible for you to actually speak to someone online when you have a problem with a return. Just an AI bot. Terrible. Great service, decent selection of clothing, although not a fan of recycled materials (feels course and not soft at all). The heels are very comfortable.

I had notification that my 3 orders were going to be delivered today. I received an email saying that delivery was attempted, but nobody was home. In the past, needed to reset password, no password reset email sent. Just the absolute worst company, never used to be like this. Will never shop with them again.

I bought two dresses in the sale to try on at home as i was in a rush. Asked about the returns period and was told 14 days later. No other information displayed at the counter. Was given no other information by the manager who was serving me.

Neither dress suited. Returned to the same store 3 days layer to be informed that:

1) Sale items are exchange only

2) They don't do gift cards

3) That if I had wanted to know this, I should have looked up the policy online before buying anything in store. Failing that, it said so on my receipt (HOW DOES THAT HELP?) 

4) It was also pointed out to me that there was a small black plaque with a QR code on it, and  'questions about returns?' written above. It was below eye level and had not been pointed out to me prior to me purchasing the items. 

5) They're not legally obliged to tell me. 

I didn't make trouble for the staff, what would have been the point? They're just doing their jobs. I'd bet my bottom dollar they've been told to obfuscate this information so people spend money they can't reclaim. 

New Look has never been a high class establishment,  but this takes them from cheap and cheerful high street staple to scummy shop to be avoided at all costs.

Tired of companies like this. Ever since the COVID drama, they've taken full advantage and really downgraded customer service. Virtually all of the clothing companies and retailers in general have.

This is so they give you less time and end up keeping your money.  New Look is most definitely going downhill.  No wonder the stores are closing down.

Purchased an item online, unfortunately did not fit so returned to store.. I was instructed to used the returns box which I found rather awkward and frustrating. My tracking states in transit… where on earth are they going to that takes so long? Come on New Look sort this out as this is not acceptable.m

Absolute disgusting customer service.

Never had an issue with New Look until now but it’s super frustrating that they just don’t come back to me!! Order number HM808431369.

Missing refund
