Completely devastating. 
Strongly advised and recommend not to buy anything from New Look.

Been a loyal customer of New Look from the very first day they opened their first store in Birmingham. Always love shopping in store and online as their clothes are acceptable for the price point and you can wear up to the minute fashion without breaking the bank. Having said all that, that’s where the positives end for me. I’ve watched this retailer / brand go downhill overnight. The staff in their stores are incredibly unhelpful and miserable. Don’t get me started on their appalling returns “so called” policy. What business of New Looks size and popularity only offers their loyal customers a faceless A1 Chatbot to raise issues with only to find you’ve got to wait 3-5 days (and much much longer in my experience) to get any support and even then you don’t get the help you want. The online returns experience is not for the faint hearted. It’s bad enough not being able to return online goods in store like most other retailers of their calibre offer but to have to go through the palaver of going through each item your returning with reasons of why, trekking to your local store to return as you have to pay to have a courier service. When you arrive at the store to drop off your returns no member of staff takes it off you, oh no, they point to a metal box with a drawer where you have to leave your returned items in where you walk away praying that New Looks warehouse will receive your items back and at some point if ever you’ll get your refund. What an absolute nightmare from a customers perspective. Whoever devised that method needs sacking they’ve not considered the customer one bit and put their needs at the heart of it. It’s a case of we don’t care, your fault if you’ve returned items and we’ll do our utmost to make you wait and suffer the anguish as to wether you’ll see your refund or not in the next whenever!!!  On several occasions I’ve returned items of the same order in the same bag, been refunded for maybe two out of the four sent back but nothing for the remainder. How can that happen??!!  They are clearly making money off the back of their loyal online shoppers. How is it we have to pay upfront before receiving the goods but we don’t get a penny back for weeks, months or if at all when we return items back. It’s an absolute nightmare and disgrace that New Looks have chosen to put profits before their customers needs. Without their customers they don’t and won’t have a business at this rate. In the awful economy climate we all find ourselves in, we customers can’t afford to shop at places like this. I’d rather spend that little bit extra and go to a competitor retailer who cares about their customers and who offers a more robust returns policy and service both instore and online. You’ve been warned New Looks so take heed before you become another retailer that’s lost from our high streets forevermore.

I didn't know sale items only had a 14 day return time. These items were bought during the Christmas period, so I thought they would have the same return policy as other Christmas purchases. I will think twice before ordering for next Christmas!

I returned an online item on 30/12/2025 back to the store , have not received a refund and can’t get a reply on messages or emails. I’m now trying my bank. 
I’m a new look customer but this has really put me off.

I recently visited the New Look store in Watford with my two young children and had a very uncomfortable experience. The manager, Kim, closely monitored us and gave my children dirty looks, even though they were in a stroller. This made us feel unwelcome and scrutinized.

When I approached the till to address my concerns, I was surprised to learn that Kim was the manager. Her dismissive attitude left me shocked. Retail environments should be inclusive and welcoming, and it's disappointing to see such behavior from management. I hope New Look takes this feedback seriously.

If only I could give zero stars! On opening my click and collect order (which was in an opaque bag) I discovered it was the wrong item. I ordered online then decided to cancel within the 20mins allowed, but the system just didn’t work. Tried several times. No way of speaking to a human about this.

⁸Absolutely shocking returns policy. I'll never order online again after this.

Had to put 1 star as no option for zero.

Absolutely shocking will never shop there again , worst refund experience ever.

I’ve been a loyal customer for years, but this recent experience has completely changed my view of this company. I bought a jacket during the Black Friday period as a Christmas gift for my daughter, who was away at university. She only received it on New Year’s Day, and we tried to return it just a few days later. The return date had passed by literally a couple of days, yet the store refused to show even the slightest bit of flexibility.

The jacket was unworn, still tagged, and in perfect condition. I explained the situation clearly, but it made absolutely no difference. I’ve now had countless emails back and forth with customer service, and every single reply has been the same cold, scripted response with zero willingness to help. Not even a store credit was offered — something that would have kept the money with them anyway.

Most retailers extend returns through January during the Christmas period, so I genuinely thought this store would do the same. Instead, I was met with rigid policies and no empathy whatsoever.

For a brand I’ve supported for so long, the lack of basic customer care is honestly shocking. After this experience, I’ll be taking my business elsewhere, and I’d strongly advise others to think twice before shopping here.

Most Useless Company Ever

 However since then finally contacting me, they issued my refund straight away as apparently my order got lost in transit and gave me 20% off and free delivery for my next order.

Visited metro centre Gateshead,,,to take few things back I had ordered on line,,young girl in beige jumper on the cash till was amazing,so helpful,,,helped me navigate how t do the return process on my phone,which took a little time as I'm not great  with technology,, even though there was quite a queue,,so polite, friendly,and patient

This has been one of the most stressful and misleading shopping experiences I’ve ever had.

I ordered items that looked great on the website, but what I received was completely different — both in quality and appearance. The items were not cheap, so I expected them to match what was advertised. I contacted the seller immediately upon delivery and sent photographic proof of the differences.

The seller initially told me I didn’t need to return anything and that a refund had already been issued as of June 6th. They told me several times to check my account — but no refund ever came.

Then after weeks of back-and-forth emails, they changed their story and told me I now needed to return the items to a U.S. address (1175 Florence Columbus Rd, Unit B) — and to do so at my own cost.

This is completely unacceptable for several reasons:
	•	The items are not as described
	•	The seller lied about issuing a refund
	•	They’re now forcing me to pay international return shipping for their own mistake

To make matters worse, even PayPal only told me to follow the seller’s return instructions, which means I must spend more money out of pocket just to get my refund. This feels like a trap, and it’s been an absolute nightmare to deal with.

Avoid this seller. It’s not worth the hassle, risk, or stress.

The NEXT App has improved considerably. It would be nice to see a few more Next own designs, but there are many smart brand alternatives aswell. The Sale section is easy to navigate, but it would be helpful to zoom in on items before 'adding to bag'. Having a detailed look from there before deciding would be helpful. I usually add to bag and click on the item so that I can zoom in and see it more clearly. It would save time not to have to do that, as certain sizes sell out very quickly. Customers know what they are looking for, or what they find different and smart in clothing. Sizing can be a bit hit and miss, even with the size guides in place. Obviously, we are all different shapes and it depends on how a garment or shoe is manufactured. Not all clothing sizes are generous, trousers are my worst nightmare. I don't see why I have to go one or even two sizes up. I don't like to have to return items, but on occasion, I might like an item of clothing, and when I try it on, it doesn't suit me at all! 
Buying boots is also a nightmare. I am actually a size 6 but I have to go for a 7, sometimes even an eight. A size 7 is a European 41, some manufacturers class it as a 40. I tend to make sure that I go for the 41 or higher. The wider sizes are available in some designs, but if they are knee high boots, the calves/ankle areas remain narrow. It seems to be just the foot that is made wider. However, wider versions change the look of a boot. They dont seem to look as streamlined. I'm always looking to see new styles and items that Next have to offer. They also have an account facility, which helps greatly to spread the cost. The Home section has some lovely items, especially the Sofa designs which are particularly well made and very comfortable.

H&M , my order was delivered to the wrong address. Evri, photo clearly shows its not my front door or my arm, hand ( male, covered in thick black hair and what look like track marks) taking the parcel, I'm female, no hair on arms and nice clear skin !

Mean while , I paid £101.60 for goods never received, and I think H & M expect folk to just meekly say OK then and walk away. So, I tried Revolver, but H & M have got that covered, when you submit documents of proof and any other communication, H & M email you saying they cannot respond to this due to security reasons ! How considerate of them or maybe its more like calculated ? So, my bank is being very understanding, almost as though this is not new to them ! H and M have untill 15th March to give me back my money and then the bank will refund me and armed with the photo graphic evidence of my front door and pale, hair free, track mark free arm and hand, I think H and M might have an uphill struggle trying to prove that I took delivery of a parcel from Evri on 9th March at 18.34. So the moral of this tale, avoid H and M like the plague. Avoid placing online orders with companies that use Evri for deliveries. Email
Subject
Formal Complaint Regarding Online Order Cancellation and In-Store Pickup Experience

Dear H&M Customer Experience Team,
I am writing to formally share a very disappointing experience I recently had with an online order placed for in-store pickup.

On Tuesday, I purchased several items online for pickup at a store located approximately two hours from my home, with the clear intention of collecting the order on Saturday. At no point during the checkout process was there any visible or explicit notice indicating that the order would be automatically cancelled if not collected within three days. Having already paid for the items, I reasonably understood that they would be held for me until the weekend, as many customers work during the week and rely on weekend availability for pickups.

To my surprise, when I arrived at the store on Saturday, I was informed that my order had been cancelled and that the staff could not release the items to me — despite the fact that:

The merchandise was physically present in the store

No refund had yet been processed to my account

I had received no notification or communication that my order was at risk of cancellation

This created a confusing and frustrating situation where items I had already paid for were denied to me without prior warning.
I then offered to repurchase the same items on the spot, assuming a reasonable accommodation would be made to honour the online pricing I had already paid. Unfortunately, I was told that the store could not offer the online prices, effectively penalizing me for a policy that had never been clearly communicated in the first place.

This experience felt deeply inconsistent with reasonable customer expectations and with the level of service one would expect from a global brand. When a customer has paid for an item, there is an implicit understanding that it will be available for pickup within a reasonable timeframe — particularly when no clear communication suggests otherwise.

The lack of transparency, the absence of notifications, and the unwillingness to provide a practical resolution in store left me extremely disappointed. I hope this feedback can prompt a review of both the communication around pickup policies and the flexibility offered to customers when issues like this arise.

I would appreciate your review of this matter and a response regarding how this situation can be appropriately addressed.

Sincerely,
A very loyal customer

I’m leaving this review to document my experience with H&M Australia’s loyalty program, so other consumers are aware.

In January 2026, I submitted multiple product reviews in good faith based on H&M’s publicly available membership information, which states that each approved review earns 5 points. There was no mention on the Australian website of any monthly cap or review limit.

Despite several confirmations from customer service that my reviews were received, 40 points were not credited. Over the course of multiple emails and a live chat session, I was given inconsistent explanations, including a “monthly cap” policy that I could not find publicly disclosed anywhere.

After issuing a formal final deadline and subsequently lodging a complaint with Consumer Affairs Victoria, the missing 40 points were finally manually credited. However, H&M has still not provided any public link or documentation showing where these loyalty point limitations are disclosed to consumers.

While the points were eventually added, the resolution only occurred after escalation to a consumer protection authority. I believe loyalty program terms and limitations should be clearly and transparently disclosed upfront to avoid misleading customers.

I hope H&M improves transparency around its membership program so customers can make informed decisions.

They constantly speak in Spanish around others so no one else knows what’s going on. If you’re not in the circle, expect to be treated differently, ignored, or targeted.

They hired someone who doesn’t speak a single word of English to work on the sales floor. I tried to work with her during truck and she didn’t understand even basic words. But because she’s friends with Myriam, she got the job and they just put her on early shifts so she doesn’t have to talk to customers.

One of the managers, Kevin, had a sexual relationship with a coworker and nothing was done. I was friends with that same coworker and suddenly Kevin started nitpicking everything I did. I reported it but of course nothing happened. It was swept under the rug just like everything else.

Illeana is a nightmare. She’s screamed at customers, chased them out of the store, and once pulled out her phone and started recording me while yelling in my face. She lied about it afterward. I asked Myriam to review the footage and she told me they “can’t even look at the cameras.” I even contacted HR and sent them a detailed report and they never responded. Nothing was done.

Illeana also drives recklessly into the parking lot when she’s late and nearly hit my child when I had to bring him with me for a few minutes one day. That alone should have gotten her fired, but again, nothing.

They treat Tamara, one of the only good managers, like she’s below them. She’s constantly stressed because she’s picking up all the slack while everyone else in the clique coasts through. She’s kind, professional, and actually listens to employees, but they dump on her because she’s not part of their friend group.

I got written up for being one minute late. Literally one minute. Meanwhile, other people call out all the time, show up late, and nothing happens. I even had sick time deducted from INFOR and they still tried to write me up for it. When I pointed that out, they said they’d ignore that one, but still counted two others to justify firing me. Makes no sense. I’ve seen people with way more callouts and absences who are still working there with no write-ups. They just wanted me gone.

I also had an issue with one of Myriam’s friends during truck. I was working alone while the other two helped each other. A coworker who wasn’t even management tried telling me what to do even though I was following orders from an actual manager. She got loud and stormed off. I told the manager what happened and that I was sick of the favoritism, of always being the one left out while they protected their friends. Her response was basically to take my break and move on.

One of their friends who used to work there still comes in and kisses a manager on the lips at the register. It’s all personal relationships and no professionalism. It’s honestly disgusting to watch happen in a store setting.

Other coworkers felt the same way but were afraid to speak up. After I reported Illeana, I started getting written up for the tiniest things. It was clear I was being pushed out. People even told me they saw it happening. This place is high school drama, gossip, and abuse of power all rolled into one. The stress it caused me mentally and physically was awful. Between the favoritism, lack of support, constant write-ups for things others weren’t punished for, and being treated like an outsider, this was the worst job I’ve ever had.

Avoid this store completely. Whether shopping or working, it’s nothing but problems.

I’m very disappointed with the quality of a pair of shoes I bought from Zara in December. After only a few months of normal use, the shoes are already coming apart on the sides. This level of durability is far below what I would expect from a brand like Zara.

I placed an online order with Zara on March 2 for items I specifically needed before St. Patrick’s Day. Unfortunately, the courier Zara chose, TForce Logistics, has completely failed to deliver the package.

TForce repeatedly claims they attempted delivery while also saying they cannot deliver to my address and asking for an alternate address. At the same time, they continue to promise delivery within 48 hours — a timeline that has now been repeated multiple times without any actual delivery taking place.

When I asked if I could simply pick up the parcel from their warehouse to resolve the issue myself, they refused to provide the warehouse location or allow pickup. As a result, I am left relying on a courier that clearly cannot resolve the situation.

At this point, the order will now arrive after St. Patrick’s Day, which defeats the entire purpose of the purchase.

What is equally disappointing is Zara’s response when I contacted customer service. I explained the situation and the failed delivery timelines, yet no effort was made to acknowledge the inconvenience or offer any form of restitution. In my experience, every other clothing retailer I’ve dealt with has offered a small goodwill gesture, such as a discount code for a future purchase, when delays like this occur. Zara refused to offer anything.

Customers should not have to chase down a courier for weeks, be denied the option to pick up their own package, and then receive no acknowledgment from the retailer when the delivery service they selected fails to meet basic timelines.

Zara should reconsider its partnership with TForce Logistics and improve how customer service addresses situations where orders are significantly delayed through no fault of the customer.

My close friends young daughter went into Zara to seek safety from a group of people threatening her, for no fault of her own. Security then escorted her back out of the shop - straight into the same group who had been following & threatening her inside the shop.

Within moments she was violently attacked. Her head was repeatedly slammed against the concrete while people stood around watching. This all happened right by the store and would clearly be on CCTV.

What makes it even worse is that the security guards who escorted her out then stood there and watched the attack take place. They didn’t intervene. They didn’t stop it. They didn’t help her. They simply stood there.

During the attack, the people involved were even heard debating smashing her head with roller blades, but decided against it because they “didn’t want to get their clothes dirty.” That alone shows the level of cruelty involved in what happened.

At no point did anyone ask if she was safe.  
At no point did anyone offer help.  
At no point was she kept inside somewhere safe or given the chance to call her parents.

Instead, she was escorted out into a situation where she was clearly at risk.

Fortunately 2 students were able to take her into their flat for safety - but only after this vicious attack had taken place

Surely basic safeguarding would mean keeping a young person inside somewhere safe if there are threats being made inside and outside? Or calling parents. Or even the police if necessary.

I genuinely want to know if people think this is acceptable behaviour from store security.

Zara really needs to review their CCTV footage and explain how this was allowed to happen.

Because no young person should ever be put in a situation like this.

Please make this known as this cannot happen again!

I visited the Zara store at Central Milton Keynes on 12 March 2026 at approximately 7:00 PM regarding a perfume I had purchased about two weeks earlier. Before buying the fragrance, I clearly explained to a member of staff that I am allergic to one of the similar-looking perfumes and asked whether I would be able to return the item if I experienced an allergic reaction. I was assured that I could bring it back if that happened.

Unfortunately, after using the perfume, I did have an allergic reaction. When I returned to the store, the manager informed me that the item could not be returned because it had been opened and used. While I understand store policies regarding opened products, the situation was disappointing because I relied on the advice given to me by a member of staff before making the purchase.

During the interaction, I explained that the issue was not about the money and that I would simply keep the product and give it to someone else, but I requested that staff avoid giving misleading advice to customers in the future. The manager responded by saying, “It’s only about £26 and I’m not going to fight you over £26,” which made me feel quite small as a customer.

I believe customers should be able to rely on the guidance provided by staff when making purchases, and I hope this feedback helps improve communication and customer service going forward.

I had one of the most frustrating online shopping experiences with Zara.

In November 2025, during the Black Friday campaign, I placed an order that included clothes and a pair of sneakers. The order was split into two deliveries. Most items arrived within about two weeks, but the sneakers never did.

In January I contacted Zara and was assured that the order was still being processed and that the product would be delivered. In February, after I contacted them again, they offered a refund, but I said I preferred to wait for the item since it had already been promised. They reassured me that the sneakers would indeed be delivered.

Two weeks ago - already in March - I was suddenly informed that the product could not be delivered after all and that they could only offer a refund.

At this point it had already been FOUR months since the order was placed. I asked for some kind of reasonable compensation for the time wasted and the misleading communication. Zara initially offered a 10% discount. Ten percent. This doesn't even cover the -30% discount that was available when I originally placed the order.

After I complained about this in their chat, they called me and once again offered... 10%. After I pointed out how inadequate that was, the "improved" offer was 15%.

Four months of waiting, repeated assurances that the item would be delivered, and the final outcome is a refund plus a symbolic discount that does not even compensate for the original promotion.

This is not just poor logistics. It is poor customer care and extremely disappointing communication.

I expected far better from a global brand like Zara.

For the past two months I’ve been contacting Zara customer service through chat. Each time I was told the case would be escalated and someone would follow up, but no one ever did. Today I was finally told the item was “not received,” which is frustrating because from a customer’s perspective there is no way to prove every item was inside the package once it leaves your hands. It appears to be a warehouse discrepancy, yet the customer is left responsible.

The amount itself isn’t significant, but the principle and the lack of follow-up from customer service are disappointing. After years of shopping with Zara, I expected better support.

My advice to other customers: if you return items by mail, keep detailed records and be cautious when returning multiple items in one package. If there’s a discrepancy, it may be very difficult to resolve.

On December 28, 2025, I made a purchase at Zara Osijek, paid by debit card. Since the store did not have my size, the item was ordered from another location.
The order was cancelled the very next day.

I requested a refund to my card, as the purchase had been paid that way. In the store, I was told this was not possible and that Zara would automatically refund the money within 14 days.

After 14 days, no refund was received.

What followed were more than 20 days of emails and chat conversations, repeatedly providing the same information, sending official bank confirmations (including ARN), and receiving contradictory explanations. Some messages were simply ignored, without any response at all.

The most absurd part: after 23 days, I was told that the refund must be processed in the physical store because Zara “does not have a system to issue the refund.”
This was exactly what I requested on day one — and was refused at that time.

The refund itself was completed in the store in 5 minutes, and the money was returned to my account two days later.

The amount was only €20, but the experience clearly shows a serious lack of coordination, accountability, and respect for the customer.
Being left without money, without clear information, and being sent in circles for almost a month is unacceptable for a brand of this scale. Customers deserve transparency, timely communication, and consistent procedures — especially when the issue is entirely on the retailer’s side.

NO Stars for Zara shopping online.

I ordered over 300 euro of goods in winter sale on 30th December and opted for a locker delivery. 3 boxes and one was empty.

Twenty three days later I am still trying to get fully refunded for the nearly 200 euros of goods missing. They eventually dismissed me by sending me a refund for 81.90euro - not quite correct! 
That is, 115.91 euro under refunded. 
Zara offers the despairing customer a tiny window to type in and wait for an assistant to ‘review the information’ and God forbid you don’t reply to them instantly or they will ‘End the conversation’. They do use Bots but then pass you to a real assistant (hmm, maybe). I have just been told they are reviewing my case (again) and it will take up to 48 hours to investigate it. Last time they told me 48 hours would be the timeframe for an answer I had to contact them again a week later as I heard nothing and, once again, there was no record of our previous exchanges or understanding of the problem. 
So I have had to keep retyping the long reference numbers for each item missing. It is easy to see their approach as deeply cynical in the hope the benighted customer will give up and go away as most people just do not have the time to challenge this. 
100 euro is small potatoes, but I am sure that if enough people just give up on their problems getting a refund, it might boost Zara’s appearance as a well run and profitable company. 
But their model is despicable and a wholly unpleasant experience in which the complainant is forced into a helpless position. We are not allowed to telephone or email them - what a pass we have come to! Is this symptomatic of a wide malaise, or what?

I want to share my experience so other shoppers understand what they may encounter.

While using the SHEIN app, I was repeatedly shown a promotion stating things like:

• “Checkout to get instant $800”
• “Pick $0.10 more to get $800 back”
• Countdown timers pressuring you to complete the purchase.

The promotion strongly implies that a very small purchase unlocks a large reward.

However, after completing the process, the “$800” turned out to be a bundle of conditional coupons that expire quickly and require additional purchases to use. These conditions were not clearly disclosed on the promotion screen itself. The interface emphasizes the large reward amount while the actual limitations are difficult to access during the promotion flow.

I have screenshots of the promotion showing how the offer is presented but I cannot attach here unfortunately.. 

My concern is not about discounts existing — it’s about how the promotion is displayed. When a promotion prominently advertises “instant $800” or “$800 back,” the material conditions should be clearly visible at the point where the claim is made.

Consumers deserve clear and transparent offers, especially when money is involved.

Very disappointing experience for a long time Shein customer.

Order: GSO15614U005WJ6

This was never communicated during the return process. If I had known I would receive nothing back, I would never have returned the items.

In the past, Shein has clearly shown when a coupon affects the refund amount, but this time there was no warning at all. It feels misleading to only inform customers after the items have been returned.

I’ve ordered from SHEIN many times, and overall it’s still my go-to for trendy pieces at really competitive prices. Compared to other similar online retailers, their pricing is hard to beat, and I’ve been pleasantly surprised by the quality of most of the clothing considering the cost.

Sizing can be a little inconsistent at times. Most items fit as expected, but I’ve definitely had a few pieces that were a bit off from the sizing guide, so I always double-check measurements and reviews before ordering.

One small frustration is that occasionally the total at checkout ends up slightly different than what I expected if a discount doesn’t apply the way I thought it would. It doesn’t happen every time, but it’s something to watch.

Shipping has been hit or miss. I’ve had orders arrive earlier than expected, and I’ve also had some come later than projected. It really just depends.

One thing I genuinely enjoy is their Puppy Keep game in the app. I actually love feeding the dog daily and working my way up to the $30 credit, which I’ve successfully earned a few times. It does require consistent daily participation to reach that goal, and none of the other promo-type games have really paid off for me the same way. But Puppy Keep has been worth it.

I've also participated in their Affiliate program, which allows you to get credits or cash payouts when friends and family use your link to order. 

Overall, despite a few minor drawbacks, the pricing, selection, and fun reward options keep me coming back to SHEIN.

Reply to Shein

Hello SHEIN Team,
Thank you for your previous response and for suggesting I use the in-app support. Unfortunately, the chatbot (robot) only provides generic answers and there's no option to reach a live agent, which makes resolving issues very difficult.
I've been a loyal customer since your early days in 2021 — I even left a positive Google review calling SHEIN the "best app forever" back then. However, the app has changed a lot, and these recent experiences are really disappointing and frustrating.
Here are the specific problems I'm facing:
Price discrepancies and unexpected increases: Items show very low prices on the product page or in suggestions (e.g., around NZ$2-3), but when added to the cart or at checkout, the total jumps significantly. Today, my cart was totaling around NZ$57 before finalizing, but the invoice became NZ$72.69.then show me you can add more so I realize i forgot to put shoe lace When I added one small NZ$2 item to try and adjust, it triggered extra shipping charges (around NZ$8), pushing the total even higher. This feels misleading.
Pressure tactics: Constant urgent messages like "1 Item Almost sold out!", "Last Chance!", "Only 2 left", "Last 10 hours", and "Buy now at this price" create unnecessary stress and make browsing feel manipulative rather than enjoyable.
Points system: Accumulating and using SHEIN points no longer feels rewarding or helpful. The value is low, and it doesn't make up for the other issues.
Support experience: There's no easy way to talk to a real person — everything routes through the robot, which doesn't solve anything properly.
As a long-time loyal customer who used to love shopping on SHEIN, it's sad to see these changes pushing people away. I understand promotions and dynamic pricing exist, but transparency would help a lot. Could you please escalate this to a supervisor or provide a proper explanation/solution? Maybe a goodwill gesture like extra points, a discount code, or clarification on how prices/shipping really work in NZ?
I'd really appreciate a human response and hope things can improve so I can continue shopping with you.
Thank you for your time and understanding.
Irfan Memon (NZ)

It’s just been handled so badly when I purchased a product that will have to be thrown in the bin!

IN THE STYLE ....I ordered an item from your company via Debenhams . Four weeks ago I sent you my return however no refund and klarna still want instalments . What a sham of a company . If you pay by credit card . You can get your money back on section 75 . This company collaborates with klarna and clear pay to make it an affordable way for customers to get items however , Debenhams are using this to deceive potential customers.
The illegal returns policies they stand by of taking up 4 weeks to refund is against the law . Maximum 14days .
The company is taking advantage of the vulnerable by offering installments .
I have two items I have returned . Unfortunately Royal Mail made an error with the tracking numbers . never the less both items were returned and I have proof of the returns . Only for integrity to be questioned by one of their customer service agents .
Not only was this lady belligerent but also stated her company would not be refunding for the items I returned Due to a postal error . Thank you Danielle .
What kind of company employs staff to abuse the consumers and refused them their refund . To me this is legalised mugging .
They then allow klarna and clear pay to take this money . 
It use to be a great company but now are just scamming their customers .
This company deserves no stars 🌟I know this might not be an option for some but it gets results - Sue ...Debenhams through the small claims even if it's a very small amount . It will cost you £70 to apply online however you add this amount to the money your are claiming for and Debenhams will not only have to refund your original sum your claiming for but will have to pay you the £70 application fees . 
Also , if you've paid them by credit card . Section 75 of the act covers you for false advertising and what they advertise is fake so the credit card will chase them after reimbursing you . 
In addition to this . Do not return if you've paid by credit card just state to them " The Goods are ready for you collect " it is then Debs who have to arrange collection under the credit card section 75 if the don't . You - have even more leverage to claim money back .

Utterly disgusting and embarrassing how ITS treat their customers. After my partner writing a review about our TERRIBLE experience, he was replied to and asked to send an email to resolve the issue. SHOCK HORROR, no reply to the email. The replies on trust pilot are just for show, don’t expect your issue to be resolved.

This company have had hundreds of pounds off us and now they have both the items and the money as they have failed to refund for the return. I have asked for the items to either be returned or my money back and have had neither. 

ITS - you should be ashamed of yourself and from doing my own research I have become aware that I am not the only person who has experienced this with them. Taking peoples hard earned money and then failing to even communicate with them. Nothing but a gang of glorified thief's.

Hoping a review on here may get someone’s attention on In The Style to eventually reply to me, but I’m not holding my breath. I personally would not recommend ordering from here as it is risky. This has happened to me before and luckily was sorted but this time seems they are not coping well and ignoring it but still taking orders and money from customers.

I placed an order on the 10th of February. I received an email confirmation immediately and, shortly after, another email to say my order had been dispatched. It said the delivery time frame was 2-3 days.
 
On the 15th of February, when my order had not arrived I referred back to the dispatch email to see if there was any tracking info to see where the parcel was. Unfortunately, the tracking link in the dispatch email did not work so I emailed ITS Customer Services. Someone called Luke replied to say that my package had been delivered on the 12th. 
It had not been delivered.

I emailed back to say that I didn't have the package and asked for some more information. On the 17th February, he replied with a working Royal Mail Tracking link that meant I could view a photo 'proof of delivery'. From the picture, it looks as though the parcel has been left in a shed with lots of tools. 
I do not have a shed. Wherever the parcel has been left - it is not on my property.

I have emailed Luke back to explain this and ask how they can help me find the package - I asked if there were any further details he could give (sometimes DPD and Evri can give you a pin on a map to where it was left) and he emailed back to say that he had 'raised an investigation' with the courier and would come back to me in 72 hours. 
He did not. 

I have not heard back from In The Style since. 

I emailed again on the 20th to ask if they had heard back from the courier with information as to where my parcel was. Explaining again that it is not at my home, the picture shows a shed and I do not have a shed. I asked them to resolve my issue or refund me. No reply. 

I emailed again on the 25th, asking for a refund. I have not received my order. Plain and simple.  They could have sent me a replacement which I would have happily accepted but now I just want my money back. 

It seems they are happy to reply until they realise there is a problem and when its clear a refund is due they prefer to ignore you. 

It's a shame as I liked this company. I thought they were one of the good guys but it seems that they are a dishonest company with poor customer service who choose to ignore you when a real issue arises.

If I don't hear back from them today I will be contacting my back to see if a chargeback is possible and I certainly won't be using them again.

I ordered an outfit from In The Style on 12th November. This item was delivered to the wrong address, I understand this is not In The Styles Fault, however the customer service I received when trying to get a refund as I didn’t receive my Parcel was shocking. I was treated like I was lying from the start. I understand being cautious and was happy to help show it had been delivered to the wrong house through the GPS location and photo of my actual front door. But instead they just continued to indicate I was lying and refused to progress with anything. After contacting Royal Mail myself and them saying they had delivered to the wrong house I went back to ITS and was again told there was nothing they could do. Only after raising this myself further with Royal Mail and back again with ITS did they say they would look into a refund within two weeks. I waited two weeks but had no response. I had to raise this again but still with no response. After another week I chased again until I was finally given a refund on 18th February. Three months to get a refund for an item I never received is not acceptable. I will say Luke who replied to me last message was the first person to actually apologize and is the only reason I’m not giving one star. Thank you Luke for treating me like a real person. I won’t be shopping with ITS again.
